Note: Power price band; LED7 lit when price band is Low

Sensor: Coffee bean level state machine (cm internally)
Note: Display only shows "Bean Lo " while a low warning is active; auto-cycle skip-rule jumps to SCREEN_DATETIME otherwise so the user only sees the screen when there's something to act on. Cm value is still published to HA as sensor.kitchen_detectorer_espresso_bean_level for diagnostics. Warning trips when the reading stays in the 5..12 cm band continuously for BEAN_RELIABLE_MS (10 s) - one grind cycle - filtering out transient bean shifts during a refill.

Sensor: 3 calendars (Felix=school1 with prefix F. / Isolde=school2 with prefix I. / handl_f no prefix)
LeftLabel: HHMM static for normal events; cal-prefix (e.g. "F.  ") static for midnight all-day events; nothing for 8am-exact events
RightFormat: title scrolls leftward over the static slot via scroll_with_static_prefix; eventually obscures the static slot as the last 8 chars settle across the display. Title is body-only when the cal prefix is in the static slot; otherwise the cal prefix is inlined as ""F. <body>"" / ""I. <body>"" so the user always knows whose event it is.
SkipIf: active in window 19:00 prev-day -> end_time and force_calendar_until expired
Note: Cycles through every active event one per 3.5s. Button 5 short press forces this screen visible for 5 s even when empty (shows noEvent). HA serialises all-day events as starts at 00:00 - we treat those specially by using the cal prefix as the static slot instead of '0000'. shorten_title strips leading 'Felix '/'Isolde ' and abbreviates Idrott->Gym, Simning/Swim->Swim, Ridlekis->Riding. Timestamps with Z/+HH:MM use the explicit offset; naive timestamps use the live local UTC offset derived from id(homeassistant_time).now() instead of mktime.
Loop return temp with C unit

LeftLabel: scrolling "Day max XX.XC RAIN 4hr XX.XC" / "Ngt min XX.XC SUN 4hr XX.XC"
RightFormat: left-scrolls over 3 s across all 8 digits
Note: Day max before 14:00; tonight min from 14:00 onwards. Right number is forecast_outside_4h (next ~4 h). Each value passes through std::isfinite() so an unavailable / NaN input_number renders as ' --C' instead of leaking 'NaN' into the scroll. Condition word is mapped from openweathermap_condition (clear-night/cloudy/fog/hail/lightning(-rainy)/partlycloudy/pouring/rainy/snowy(-rainy)/sunny/windy(-variant)/exceptional) to a 5-6 char ALL-CAPS abbreviation and dropped entirely when the sensor has no state.

RightFormat: sanitised content scrolls leftward over the static HHMM via scroll_with_static_prefix at NOTIFY_SCROLL_MS_PER_CELL=200 ms/cell so longer notifications take longer; multi-notification cases prepend "N of M " before scrolling
Note: Content is picked message-first then title-first then alarm-level fallback - most HA persistent_notification.create calls leave title empty and put the readable text in message. HHMM is captured by the notification_ids_csv on_value handler the first time each ID appears; previously seen IDs keep their original HHMM via the parallel notification_times vector. Content is sanitised to [0-9 A-Z a-z space . - _] before display. A B8 short press extends the auto-cycle hold to 'scroll cells * 200 ms + 3000 ms' via notify_hold_until_ms so the entire message gets seen, plus a tail dwell, before auto-rotation resumes.
